2605.16714.title: GRID: Graph Representation of Intelligence Data for Security Text Knowledge Graph Construction
2605.16714.paper_summary: Security knowledge graphs can provide computable external memory for security agents, but constructing them from long-form cyber threat intelligence (CTI) remains difficult: LLMs often lack grounded security-domain knowledge, and end-to-end document-to-graph training is hard to supervise with cheap, stable rewards. We present GRID (Graph Representation of Intelligence Data), an end-to-end framework for security text knowledge graph construction. GRID first builds security-domain supervision from CTI articles by creating traceable article-graph alignments through graph extraction and knowledge-graph-conditioned text revision. It then turns document-to-graph learning into a scripted task bank combining four-option multi-select questions with triple-level regex matching targets, yielding more stable task-specific rewards than repeatedly scoring full graph outputs with an LLM judge. Using this supervision pipeline, we train two Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507-based 4B extractors: a primary Task-bank Reward model and a secondary End2End Reward model with LLM-as-judge precision/recall rewards. On 249 CTI articles from GRID, CASIE, CTINexus, MalKG, and SecureNLP, the Task-bank Reward model with the ontology-guided GRID extraction pipeline reaches 84.62% source-averaged precision, 64.91% source-averaged recall, and 68.53% Avg F1, achieving the best source-averaged recall and near-top Avg F1 with lower token usage and deployment cost. The End2End Reward model reaches 76.91% precision, 53.85% recall, and 58.06% Avg F1. Further analyses show that task-bank rewards can be built once offline and reused across later post-training runs, outperforming online End2End LLM-as-judge reward and weaker alternatives such as Choice-only Reward and End2End SFT without RL.
2605.16714.paper_summary_zh: 安全知識圖譜可以為安全代理提供可計算的外部記憶，但從長篇網絡威脅情報 (CTI) 建構它們仍然困難：大型語言模型 (LLMs) 通常缺乏扎實的安全領域知識，且端到端的文件到圖譜訓練難以用廉價、穩定的獎勵進行監督。 我們提出了 GRID（情報數據的圖譜表示），這是一個端到端的安全文本知識圖譜建構框架。 GRID 首先通過圖譜提取和知識圖譜條件的文本修訂，從 CTI 文章中建立安全領域的監督，創建可追蹤的文章-圖譜對齊。 然後，它將文件到圖譜的學習轉變為一個腳本化的任務庫，結合四選一的多選問題和三層正則表達式匹配目標，產生比重複用 LLM 評判整個圖譜輸出得分更穩定的任務特定獎勵。 使用這個監督管道，我們訓練了兩個基於 Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 的 4B 提取器：一個主要的任務庫獎勵模型和一個次要的端到端獎勵模型，具有 LLM 作為評判的精確度/召回獎勵。 在來自 GRID、CASIE、CTINexus、MalKG 和 SecureNLP 的 249 篇 CTI 文章中，使用本體引導的 GRID 提取管道的任務庫獎勵模型達到 84.62% 的來源平均精確度、64.91% 的來源平均召回率和 68.53% 的平均 F1，實現了最佳的來源平均召回率和接近頂級的平均 F1，且使用的標記數量和部署成本較低。 端到端獎勵模型達到 76.91% 的精確度、53.85% 的召回率和 58.06% 的平均 F1。 進一步分析顯示，任務庫獎勵可以在離線時建立並在後續的訓練運行中重複使用，表現優於線上端到端的 LLM 作為評判的獎勵以及較弱的替代方案，如僅選擇獎勵和沒有強化學習的端到端 SFT。
2605.16714.author: Liangyi Huang et.al.
2605.16714.authors: Liangyi Huang, Zichen Liu, Fei Shao, Shang Ma, Mengshi Zhang, Zihao Chen, Yanfang Ye, Xusheng Xiao
